title: Repository Repair, Release, and Collaboration Capstone · Repository Repair Bay
summary: Recover and ship a repository containing mixed work, a conflict, a mistaken commit, a remote update, and a release deadline. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Capture repository boundaries, config origins, current operation, worktree/index state, graph, remotes, and recovery refs before repair.
Order preservation, reproduction, local repair, tests, history cleanup, and publication from least to most irreversible.
Coordinate rewritten history, conflicts, ownership, release changes, and recovery instructions with explicit affected refs and people.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v3.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

Inspect the Repository Triage trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for capture repository boundaries, config origins, current operation, worktree/index state, graph, remotes, and recovery refs before repair.

content.text: For Repository Triage, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Repository Triage precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A debug repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v3.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: git status alone captures all state needed for repository recovery.

stimulus.title: Repository Triage case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A debug repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v3.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: git status alone captures all state needed for repository recovery.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Capture repository boundaries, config origins, current operation, worktree/index state, graph, remotes, and recovery refs before repair. Artifact under review: a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Repository Triage, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-handoff-runbook-1k69i8" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Handoff Runbook into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: git-handoff-runbook-1k69i8
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable cause]
# command: choose one bounded simulator observation
# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Handoff Runbook evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Handoff Runbook assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Handoff Runbook: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v6.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Publish an accessible repository runbook covering setup, workflow, gates, release, recovery, and escalation with tested commands.
